title: Requirements Handoff
description: BA agent output consumed by all downstream agents. Defines what needs to be built, acceptance criteria, constraints, and scope boundaries.

handoff_type.description: Handoff type identifier. Always 'requirements' for this schema.

task_summary.description: One-line summary of what needs to be built. Should be specific enough that a developer can understand the goal without reading the full task description.

acceptance_criteria.description: List of TESTABLE criteria. Each criterion must be verifiable by the test agent — if you can't write a test for it, rewrite it. Bad: 'good user experience'. Good: 'login completes in under 3 seconds'.

technical_constraints.description: Existing system constraints that the developer must respect. Include framework versions, API contracts, database schemas, or architectural patterns already in place.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded items. Prevents downstream agents from wasting tokens on work that was deliberately excluded. Be specific — 'mobile support' not 'other stuff'.

affected_modules.description: File paths or module names likely to be modified. Determined by actually tracing through the codebase, not guessing.

domain_glossary.description: Key-value pairs of domain terms used in this task and their meaning in this codebase's context. Prevents downstream agents from misinterpreting domain-specific language.

estimated_complexity.description: Complexity estimate based on number of affected files, integration points, and risk of regressions. simple: 1-3 files, no integrations. medium: 4-10 files or external integrations. complex: 10+ files, multiple integrations, or high regression risk.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this handoff was produced.
